description.content: Operations for managing internet gateways that connect VPCs to the internet

description.content: Creates an internet gateway for use with a VPC. After creating the internet gateway, you attach it to a VPC using AttachInternetGateway.

Operations for managing NAT gateways for outbound internet access from private subnets

item.name: Amazon VPC Create a NAT Gateway

request.name: Amazon VPC Create a NAT Gateway

description.content: Creates a NAT gateway in the specified subnet. A NAT gateway enables instances in a private subnet to connect to the internet or other AWS services, but prevents the internet from initiating a connection with those instances.

query.description: (Required) The ID of the subnet in which to create the NAT gateway
The allocation ID of an Elastic IP address to associate with the NAT gateway
Indicates whether the NAT gateway supports public or private connectivity

item.name: Amazon VPC Create a Network ACL

request.name: Amazon VPC Create a Network ACL

description.content: Creates a network ACL in a VPC. Network ACLs provide an optional layer of security for your VPC that acts as a firewall for controlling traffic in and out of one or more subnets.

query.description: (Required) The ID of the VPC

item.name: Amazon VPC Create a Route in a Route Table

request.name: Amazon VPC Create a Route in a Route Table

description.content: Creates a route in a route table within a VPC. You must specify either a destination CIDR block or a prefix list ID. You must also specify exactly one of the targets.

The IPv4 CIDR address block used for the destination match

item.name: Amazon VPC Associate a Route Table with a Subnet

request.name: Amazon VPC Associate a Route Table with a Subnet

description.content: Associates a subnet or gateway with a route table. The subnet and route table must be in the same VPC. This association causes traffic from the subnet to be routed according to the routes in the route table.

The ID of the subnet

Operations for creating and managing Virtual Private Clouds

Creates a VPC with the specified IPv4 CIDR block. The smallest VPC you can create uses a /28 netmask (16 IPv4 addresses), and the largest uses a /16 netmask (65,536 IPv4 addresses). After creating the VPC, you can add one or more subnets.

AWS Region

Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) lets you provision a logically isolated section of the AWS Cloud where you can launch AWS resources in a virtual network that you define. VPC operations are part of the Amazon EC2 API and use the EC2 query-based API pattern.
